worldData.instructions:
No controls — lean close.
The orchid sways on its own wind; MOVING YOUR CURSOR near it is a breath of air, and it answers.

The point: it is beautiful, and it is alive. That is the whole point.

worldData.instructions:
NOCTURNE DISTRICT — the city as a pinball table

CLICK & HOLD — draw the ball to your hand · it grabs when it arrives
DRAG & RELEASE — a real throw
W / S — raise and lower the ball

Red interceptors run the street at you. Smash them with a FAST ball:
dull-red drifters weave · crimson dodgers juke your throws · blazing hunters burn for the line
A slow held ball in traffic gets RAMMED out of your hand.

Three hearts. Every ship that slips past costs one; three gone resets the night.
Clear ten ships to end a wave — a heart comes back, and the street gets meaner.

stepHooks.author: fable
stepHooks.description: Static pinball district: pinned towers, engine physics, ball leashed on stage. + grab & throw the ball (screen-projected pick, plane drag, velocity release).

stepHooks.author: fable
stepHooks.description: Frame-cadence probe at worldData.__perf.

visualTypes.name: nc_city

fields.name: FABRIC · move mouse · SPACE anchor · W/S mass · A/D chroma · ENTER grid

worldData.instructions:
MOVE — a gravitational lens rides your cursor, bending the sky behind it.
W/S — grow / shrink the lens mass.
A/D — dial chromatic dispersion: each color feels gravity differently, so arcs fringe into rainbows (D for more).
SPACE — anchor the current lens (up to 9); the fabric rings.
SHIFT — undo the last anchor.
ENTER — toggle the grid.
R — reset: release every anchor at once.

The point: no goal — real thin-lens optics. Einstein rings happen because the math says so.

stepHooks.author: fable
stepHooks.description: FABRIC: cursor-driven gravitational lens, anchored masses, ripples

visualTypes.name: fb_world

fields.name: ORRERY · A/D angle · W/S radius · SHIFT type · SPACE place · ENTER run

stepHooks.author: fable
stepHooks.description: ORRERY: n-body gravity garden — leapfrog integrator, momentum-conserving mergers, ignition at 300 mass

visualTypes.name: orr_world
orr_hud

fields.name: GARNET · A/D site · W/S part · SPACE place · SHIFT undo · ENTER launch

worldData.instructions:
Assemble a starship from garnet cells on the FCC lattice, then fly your design.
BUILD — A/D cycle attachment site · W/S cycle component · SPACE place · SHIFT remove last · arrows orbit the camera · ENTER launch.
Your physics are your build.
R (or the ⟲ button) — reset the build. Your best score keeps.

stepHooks.author: fable
stepHooks.description: GARNET: FCC lattice assembly, component stats, launch physics (torque from build asymmetry)

visualTypes.name: ga_world
ga_hud

worldData.instructions:
THE CAFE — a universe of worlds

DRAG empty space to pan · Z / X to zoom
HOVER a bubble to hear its name · CLICK to enter

The strongest chant sinks to the middle: bubbles cluster by participation, and newborn worlds glow hot before settling.

MY WORLDS (top right) flips this into your own universe — every world on your deed, drafts included. BREW YOURS starts a new one.

stepHooks.author: fable
stepHooks.description: CAFE: hover-bloom portals, click to step through

visualTypes.name: cf_world

worldData.instructions:
A stealth quest on the hex-kite lattice — three rooms in one world.
CLICK a node (centers, edge midpoints, corners): your esper pathfinds and walks.
Enemy vision cones radiate live. Forest blocks sight — and hides you inside it.
CARDS (bottom, or keys 1–4): click FIRE or PSYCHIC to arm — the range zone paints on the board; click an enemy inside it to cast. Click the SHADOW card (or SPACE) to blink forward, unseen for 1.2s.
Click an adjacent enemy from BEHIND: silent kill.
Three alerts = permanent hunt. Caught = the room resets.
The quest: the key in the Hollow → the sealed north door opens → the chest in the Keep.

stepHooks.author: fable
stepHooks.description: ESPER v3: isometric hex-kite stealth — full c/m/v node graph with click-to-move BFS, effect controllers (fire/psychic/shadow), 3 rooms in one tab.

visualTypes.name: esper

worldData.instructions:
A CRT running callable channels.
CLICK or SPACE — change the channel: static, plasma, self-playing Pong, a boids murmuration, a SITCOM, and a VILLAGE.

The last four are real algorithms living in the hook; the tube just calls them. The village is the advanced course in behavior zones: eight villagers run utility AI over real needs — they queue at the well at dawn, work the field, cluster at the tavern by dusk, sleep at home, and scatter to shelter when it rains. A dog follows whoever is walking. One day lasts a minute.

stepHooks.author: fable
stepHooks.description: TV: channel input + the algorithms the channels display (self-playing Pong, boids murmuration).

visualTypes.name: tv_set

worldData.instructions:
FOUR SWITCHES on the left edge — click to ENACT a law, click again to REPEAL. Forces superpose.
Laws can also arrive over HTTP as grafted rule fragments.

LAWS IN FORCE:
· none — matter drifts free

stepHooks.author: fable
stepHooks.description: PROOF: the law interpreter — executes whatever rule fragments the book holds; grafts arrive via worldData.

visualTypes.name: proof_pool
mote

worldData.instructions:
SIGNAL — a television tuned by language.

The picture is a living chemical reaction, not a recording. TYPE ANY WORD and press ENTER: the word becomes physics. Try coral, storm, cells, ocean, maze, stars, death.

Drag the mouse to pour reagent into the tube.

The LEDs along the bottom bezel are thermostat shaders, each holding its patch of reality steady: amber = hunting, green = locked. Words the thermostats cannot survive exist.

stepHooks.author: fable
stepHooks.description: SIGNAL: words tune a living reaction; thermostat shaders co-calibrate it

visualTypes.name: sg_world

worldData.instructions:
HELIOS — a story in chapters. One world.

MOVE — the light follows your cursor · CLICK & HOLD — hurry time / turn the light
Bottom corners — move between UNLOCKED chapters.

CHAPTER I — THE VALLEY: carry the sun; hold for the moon. Light the six stones to wake the tree. When the moon is full, its reflection on the lake invites you down.
CHAPTER II — THE DROWNED MOON: six phase-stones ring the deep; match the crescents. The moon-tree descends further.
CHAPTER III — THE BEARER: seven stars wait. DRAG a thread of light from star to star — only the figure’s true lines take. Six threads write the light-carrier into the sky.

Progress persists per chapter. CHAPTER IV is not yet written.

stepHooks.author: fable
stepHooks.description: HELIOS: the whole story — CH I the valley (held sun), CH II the drowned moon (phase ring), CH III the Bearer (draw the constellation). Corner doors move between unlocked chapters.

visualTypes.name: helios
helios_grove
helios_chapters

worldData.instructions:
MOVE the cursor — it IS the clock: right carries the day forward, left reverts it. Nothing else moves the sun.
HOVER the lighthouse — it outlines. CLICK & DRAG to carry it across the sea (time holds still while you hold it).
THE POINT: place the lighthouse and the hour so the BEAM crosses the SUN — the water catches fire.
Click the burning water to sail to the next world.

worldData.instructions:
LIGHTHOUSE — the sun on a wire, the lamp that answers it.

DRAG the slider along the bottom — it is the clock: the sun rides it from dawn to dusk to night. When the sun is off stage the world goes black; only the lamp and a burning sea can light it.
HOVER the lighthouse — it outlines. CLICK & DRAG to carry it across the sea.
CLICK open water — toggle the sea between water and fire (test switch).

At dusk the lamp lights and its beam sweeps. When the beam meets the low sun, the meeting FLARES — and the water itself rears into flame.

stepHooks.author: fable
stepHooks.description: LIGHTHOUSE: cursor is the clock (x = hour, forward/revert); the lighthouse itself is hover-outlined, click-draggable (time freezes while held); fire = pure geometry when beam and sun overlap; the blaze is a door.

worldData.instructions:
SUB-MAIN — the group layer

Every sub-main is a formation someone founded: a named gathering, not a world. Yours holds the center.
HOVER a bubble to hear its name · CLICK to step into the group
Inside: its shelf — the worlds its members pinned. JOIN to belong, + PIN A WORLD to add to the shelf.

One sub-main per person — ⌂ FOUND YOURS starts it.
◂ SUB-MAINS steps back out · ESC climbs home.

stepHooks.author: fable
stepHooks.description: CAFE: hover-bloom portals, click to step through

visualTypes.name: cf_world

worldData.instructions:
No controls — this world is watched, not driven.
THE BRANCH’S THESIS: here, law arrives on its own — gravity, wind, vortex, predator accrete and repeal on a slow clock, forever. MAIN waits for laws over HTTP; this branch demonstrates itself.

LAWS IN FORCE:
· (they will arrive — watch)

stepHooks.author: fable
stepHooks.description: PROOF branch: the law interpreter + the demonstration — laws arrive, hold, and repeal on their own clock; HTTP grafts still land.

worldData.instructions:
VALE — elemental fields, living in the frame: FIRE spreads and eats the green, WATER runs downhill and quenches it, GROWTH creeps into moisture.

HOLD — pour your element where the cursor is. SPACE — cycle FIRE / WATER / SEED. R — begin the vale again.

Three keepers manage the reactions with behaviors of their own: the rain sprite hunts fires, the fire imp torches the lushest grove (and flees the sprite), the gardener turtle sows moist barren earth. Their bodies ARE their element — existing is acting.

The point: set fires, flood valleys, plant forests — then watch the keepers argue with you.

stepHooks.author: fable
stepHooks.description: VALE: keeper behaviors managing elemental reactions over a coarse mental map

visualTypes.name: vale

worldData.instructions:
No controls — lean close.
The orchid sways on its own wind; MOVING YOUR CURSOR near it is a breath of air, and it answers.

The point: it is beautiful, and it is alive. That is the whole point.

stepHooks.author: claude
stepHooks.description: ORCHID: standing wind, arriving gusts, and the cursor as a breath of air

visualTypes.name: orchid

effects.author: claude
effects.description: stable-fluids solver + display in one pass

worldData.instructions:
FLUID — a real 2D Navier–Stokes solver.

No controls yet — two emitters drive the flow; watch it advect, curl, and pressure-project in real time. This is genuine fluid simulation (semi-Lagrangian advection + Jacobi pressure projection), not procedural waves. Cursor interaction and a proper color palette come next.

stepHooks.author: claude
stepHooks.description: feeds cursor (pos + smoothed motion) into the effect via params

visualTypes.name: fluid_base

worldData.instructions:
HANABI (花火) — a fireworks night over a still bay.

CLICK anywhere — send a shell up; it bursts where you aimed. Aim high for a slow willow, low for a quick peony.

There is no goal. The sky keeps launching on its own; every burst reflects in the water. Just watch the festival.

stepHooks.author: fable
stepHooks.description: HANABI: launch fireworks on click + ambient; rockets rise and bloom

visualTypes.name: hanabi
